titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/106

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=391 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:36:58.013277+00 | translation moa=392 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:37:00.611595+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis . Ricardus de Morauilla constabularius Regis Scotie . et Willelmus filius eius et heres salutem . Sciatis nos concessisse . et hac presenti carta nostra con
firmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus nostris et omnium antecessorum et successorum nostrorum . locum in Witelei infra marginem fo
reste ad vaccariam faciendam centum uaccarum scilicet sexies uiginti . siue berchariam . quecumque eis et domui ipsorum melior et utilior uisa fuerit . libere et quiete te
nendam et possidendam ab eis in perpetuum . sine omni disturbatione nostra et omnium nostrorum . et omnium contra quos eos manutenere et eandem possessionem eis wa
rantizare potuerimus . et unam domum in qua focum faciant fratribus et pastoribus suis . et unam etiam domum in qua fenum suum ponant . Has tres domos in pu
ram et perpetuam elemosinam eisdem monachis concessimus faciendas in prefato loco de Witelei et possidendas in perpetuum . Et sciendum quod nullas alias logias facient per forestam  sed pastores eorum habebunt claias wiscatas . sub quibus custodientes aueria sua iaceant cum opus fuerit . Hiis testibus Ioceli
no episcopo Glasguensi . herberto decano de Glasgu . Thoma clerico . Godefrido de Ros . Rad' Makerel . Ricardo filio Sewar . Alano filio elzi . Gilleberto de lancastro
Ricardo filio Gilleberti . Ricardo filio Gaufridi . Willelmo dapifero episcopi Glasguensis . Magistro Rogero de neuport .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church Richard de Morville constable of the King of Scotland and William his son and heir send greeting. Know us to have granted and by this our present charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for our souls and of all our ancestors' and successors' souls, a place in Whitelee inside the limits of the forest for making a vaccary, that is, for a hundred and twenty-six cows, or a sheep-fold, whatever seems better and more useful to them and their house; to be held and possessed freely and quitely by them for ever without any disturbance from us and of all our men and of all men against whom we shall have been able to maintain them and warrant the same possession to them; and one building in which they may make a dwelling for the brethren and their shepherds and one building also in which they may put their hay. We have granted these three buildings in pure and everlasting alms to the same monks to be built in the forementioned place at Whitelee and to be possessed for ever. And it is to be known that they shall build no other dwellings in the forest, but their shepherds shall have wattled huts under which the livestock being guarded shall lie when the need arises. With these witnesses: Jocelin bishop of Glasgow; Herbert dean of Glasgow; Thomas, clerk; Godfrey de Ros; Ralph Mackerel; Richard son of Sewar; Alan son of Aelsi; Gilbert of Lancaster; Richard son of Gilbert; Richard son of Geoffrey; William the bishop of Glasgow's steward; Master Roger of Newport.
